title: Name and read whole numbers in the millions
blurb: Use place value grouping of ones, tens and hundreds to name numbers in the millions.

title: Order numbers in the millions
blurb: Arrange large whole numbers in ascending and descending order.

title: Round whole numbers to a given place value
blurb: Round large numbers to the nearest hundred, thousand, million, etc.

title: Partition, regroup and rename numbers to 1 billion
blurb: Express numbers in standard and non-standard partitioned forms up to 1 billion.

title: Express and interpret thousandths as decimals
blurb: Read and write decimals to 3 places and the place value of each digit.

title: Partition decimals using place value
blurb: Break a decimal into the sum of its place-value parts, e.g. 5.37 = 5 + 0.3 + 0.07.

title: Compare and order decimals up to 3 decimal places
blurb: Order decimals and interpret trailing zeros, e.g. 0.170 = 0.17.

title: Scale decimals by 10, 100, 1/10 and 1/100
blurb: Find numbers 10 or 100 times, or 1/10 or 1/100 of, a given decimal.

title: Approximate decimals to a given number of places
blurb: Round a decimal to a stated number of decimal places.

title: Place decimals on a number line
blurb: Locate decimals of up to 3 places on a number line.

title: Locate integers on a number line
blurb: Place positive and negative whole numbers in relation to zero.

title: Interpret integers in everyday contexts
blurb: Use negative numbers in contexts such as temperature.

title: Understand percentages as parts per 100
blurb: Recognise that % means per 100 and 100% is the whole.

title: Recall equivalent percentages, decimals and fractions
blurb: Connect benchmark values such as 1/2, 1/4, 3/4 with their decimals and percentages.

title: Find 10% of a quantity
blurb: Recognise 10% as one-tenth and use it to find 10% of an amount.

title: Find 10%, 25% and 50% of a quantity
blurb: Equate 10% to ÷10, 25% to ÷4, and 50% to halving.

title: Calculate a sale price after a discount
blurb: Find the price of an item after a 10%, 25% or 50% discount.

title: Solve addition and subtraction word problems
blurb: Interpret and solve worded addition and subtraction problems, including multistep.

title: Apply mental strategies for addition and subtraction
blurb: Use levelling, constant difference, addition for subtraction and bridging.

title: Add and subtract numbers with different numbers of digits
blurb: Use place value to add or subtract 3 or more numbers.

title: Round numbers to estimate a calculation
blurb: Round numbers appropriately to obtain an estimate.

title: Check reasonableness using estimation
blurb: Use estimation and place value to judge whether an answer is reasonable.

title: Solve multistep problems with more than one operation
blurb: Choose and combine operations to solve multistep word problems.

title: Add and subtract decimals
blurb: Add and subtract decimals up to 3 decimal places.

title: Solve word problems adding and subtracting decimals
blurb: Apply decimal addition and subtraction to worded problems.

title: Find the product of numbers
blurb: Use the term product and multiply 2 or more numbers.

title: Determine the factors of a whole number
blurb: List the factors of a number and express it as products.

title: Identify prime and composite numbers
blurb: Determine whether a number is prime, composite or neither.

title: Multiply by 10, 100, 1000 and their multiples
blurb: Use place value to multiply one-digit numbers by powers of 10 and their multiples.

title: Estimate the product of two numbers
blurb: Use multiples of 10 or 100 to estimate a product.

title: Multiply using the area model and distributive property
blurb: Partition by place value to multiply, e.g. 684×5 = (600×5)+(80×5)+(4×5).

title: Multiply by a one-digit number using the algorithm
blurb: Record multiplication by a one-digit number with a formal algorithm.

title: Factorise numbers to aid mental multiplication
blurb: Use the associative property, e.g. 12×25 = 3×4×25 = 3×100.

title: Multiply by 2-digit numbers
blurb: Multiply 2- and 3-digit numbers by 2-digit numbers using the area model and algorithm.

title: Solve multiplication word problems
blurb: Interpret and solve worded multiplication problems.

title: Model division with remainders
blurb: Use arrays or diagrams to model division where there is a remainder.

title: Use fact families to solve division with remainders
blurb: Apply known multiplication facts to divide, e.g. 27÷6 = 4 remainder 3.

title: Connect division and multiplication
blurb: Relate divisor, quotient and remainder, e.g. 25 = 4×6 + 1.

title: Estimate the result of a division
blurb: Estimate quotients when dividing by a one-digit divisor.

title: Divide large numbers by a one-digit divisor
blurb: Partition by place value and use known multiples to divide, e.g. 3248÷4.

title: Interpret remainders in division problems
blurb: Decide how to use or round a remainder in a worded problem.

title: Check reasonableness of multiplication and division
blurb: Use estimation to judge multiplication and division answers.

title: Select efficient multiplication and division strategies
blurb: Choose mental or written methods for numbers up to 4 digits by 1- and 2-digit numbers.

title: Solve rate problems with multiplication and division
blurb: Use multiplication and division to solve worded rate problems.

title: Multiply decimals by single-digit numbers
blurb: Use mental strategies to multiply benchmark decimals, e.g. 3.5×2.

title: Multiply and divide decimals by powers of 10
blurb: Shift place value to multiply or divide a decimal by 10, 100 or 1000.

title: Estimate the product of a decimal and a whole number
blurb: Estimate to judge the magnitude of a decimal multiplication.

title: Complete number sentences to find missing numbers
blurb: Find unknowns in number sentences with more than one operation.

title: Use inverse operations to solve number sentences
blurb: Rearrange a number sentence using inverse operations to find an unknown.

title: Record division as a fraction
blurb: Recognise that a÷b can be written as the fraction a/b.

title: Build a table of values from a geometric pattern
blurb: Create a table of values for a pattern involving multiples.

title: Describe a pattern of multiples in words
blurb: Describe a pattern in terms of multiplication rather than addition.

title: Find the rule relating quantities in a table
blurb: Determine the rule connecting the two rows of a table of values.

title: Apply the order of operations
blurb: Evaluate number sentences applying the agreed order of operations.

title: Use brackets to control the order of operations
blurb: Insert and interpret grouping symbols () to make a number sentence true.

title: Recognise fractions need a common whole
blurb: Understand that fractions must refer to the same whole to be compared or added.

title: Compare unit fractions to 1/2
blurb: Decide whether a unit fraction is smaller or larger than the benchmark 1/2.

title: Compare and order unit fractions
blurb: Order unit fractions (denominators 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10) on a number line.

title: Add and subtract fractions with the same denominator
blurb: Combine and find the difference of fractions sharing a denominator.

title: Subtract a fraction from a whole number
blurb: Use the complement principle, e.g. 1 − 1/3 = 2/3.

title: Solve word problems with same-denominator fractions
blurb: Apply same-denominator addition and subtraction to worded problems.

title: Interpret a fraction as a division
blurb: Read a fraction as numerator divided by denominator, e.g. 3/4 = 3÷4.

title: Order fractions with related denominators
blurb: Order common fractions with related denominators using diagrams and number lines.

title: Create and record equivalent fractions
blurb: Generate equivalent fractions using diagrams, words and notation, e.g. 6/8 = 3/4.

title: Compare fractions using area and discrete models
blurb: Represent and compare fractions of shapes and collections.

title: Reconstruct the whole from a fractional part
blurb: Given a non-unit fractional part, find the whole quantity.

title: Add and subtract fractions with related denominators
blurb: Use equivalence to add and subtract fractions like 1/4 and 1/8.

title: Find a unit fraction of a quantity
blurb: Find 1/2, 1/4, 1/5 and 1/10 of a measure or collection.

title: Find a non-unit fraction of a whole number
blurb: Use a tape diagram to find fractions like 2/5 of 30.

title: Solve word problems involving a fraction of a quantity
blurb: Apply fractions of quantities to worded problems, expressing remainders as decimals.

title: Plot points in the first quadrant
blurb: Plot and label points given coordinates, horizontal position first.

title: Read coordinates in the first quadrant
blurb: Identify and record the coordinates of given points.

title: Use metres and kilometres
blurb: Recognise 1000 m = 1 km and record distances in km.

title: Estimate lengths and distances
blurb: Choose an appropriate unit and estimate lengths and distances.

title: Calculate the perimeter of 2D shapes
blurb: Find the perimeter of squares, rectangles and triangles.

title: Determine which sides are needed for a perimeter
blurb: Decide which side lengths are required to calculate a perimeter.

title: Measure angles with a protractor
blurb: Measure angles up to 360° and record them using the degree symbol (°).

title: Estimate angles using benchmarks
blurb: Estimate the size of an angle using known angles such as 90°.

title: Construct angles with a protractor
blurb: Draw angles of up to 360° using a protractor.

title: Classify angles by size
blurb: Identify acute, right, obtuse, straight, reflex angles and revolutions.

title: Plot and read points in all four quadrants
blurb: Plot and record coordinates across all four quadrants of the number plane.

title: Describe coordinate changes under transformation
blurb: Describe how coordinates change when a point is translated or reflected across an axis.

title: Record lengths using decimal notation
blurb: Connect whole-number and decimal length, e.g. 165 cm = 1.65 m.

title: Convert between metres and kilometres
blurb: Use the decimal place value system to convert length units.

title: Compare lengths by converting to a common unit
blurb: Convert measurements to the same unit before comparing.

title: Compare perimeters of rectangles with the same area
blurb: Investigate how rectangles with equal area can have different perimeters.

title: Solve length and perimeter problems
blurb: Solve problems involving length and perimeter, including different units.

title: Recognise angle relationships in diagrams
blurb: Identify right angles, angles on a straight line and angles at a point.

title: Recognise perpendicular lines
blurb: Recognise that perpendicular lines intersect at right angles (90°).

title: Find adjacent angles that add to 90°
blurb: Use the fact that adjacent angles forming a right angle add to 90°.

title: Find adjacent angles on a straight line
blurb: Use the fact that angles on a straight line add to 180°.

title: Find angles at a point
blurb: Use the fact that angles at a point add to 360°.

title: Classify triangles
blurb: Identify equilateral, isosceles and scalene triangles.

title: Classify quadrilaterals by their properties
blurb: Compare side and angle properties of squares, rectangles, parallelograms and rhombuses.

title: Investigate symmetry of quadrilaterals
blurb: Identify line and rotational symmetry of quadrilaterals.

title: Identify regular and irregular polygons
blurb: Distinguish regular from irregular polygons.

title: Use hectares and square kilometres
blurb: Recognise when to use ha and km² and that 10 000 m² = 1 ha.

title: Calculate the area of a rectangle
blurb: Find area as length × width in cm², m² and km².

title: Relate area, perimeter and dimensions of rectangles
blurb: Recognise rectangles with the same area or perimeter can have different dimensions.

title: Describe transformations of shapes
blurb: Use the terms translate, reflect and rotate to describe transformations.

title: Dissect and rearrange shapes
blurb: Cut and rearrange one shape to form another, preserving size.

title: Find the area of composite figures
blurb: Split or rearrange an L-shaped figure to find its area.

title: Find the area of a parallelogram
blurb: Transform a parallelogram into a rectangle to find area = base × height.

title: Find the area of a triangle
blurb: Find a triangle's area as half that of a parallelogram with the same base and height.

title: Compare prisms and pyramids
blurb: Compare properties such as uniform cross-section, bases and apex.

title: Name prisms and pyramids
blurb: Name a prism or pyramid by the shape of its base.

title: Sketch 3D objects from different views
blurb: Visualise and sketch top, front and side views of 3D objects.

title: Work with nets of 3D objects
blurb: Identify, sketch and name prisms and pyramids from their nets.

title: Choose units for capacity
blurb: Select appropriate units (mL, L) to measure capacity.

title: Compare volumes using displacement
blurb: Compare volumes by observing change in water level.

title: Record capacities using decimal notation
blurb: Connect whole-number and decimal capacity, e.g. 375 mL = 0.375 L.

title: Construct prisms and pyramids
blurb: Build skeletal and solid models of prisms and pyramids from drawings.

title: Use the cubic metre
blurb: Estimate and measure larger volumes in cubic metres.

title: Describe volume as layers of cubes
blurb: Relate cubes in one layer and the number of layers to find volume.

title: Calculate the volume of a rectangular prism
blurb: Find volume in cm³ and m³ from its dimensions.

title: Relate volume and dimensions of prisms
blurb: Recognise prisms with the same volume can have different dimensions.

title: Choose units and devices for mass
blurb: Select appropriate units and devices to measure mass (g, kg, t).

title: Record masses using decimal notation
blurb: Connect whole-number and decimal mass, e.g. 3 kg 250 g = 3.25 kg.

title: Read 24-hour time
blurb: Read and use 24-hour time language.

title: Convert between 12- and 24-hour time
blurb: Convert times between am/pm and 24-hour notation.

title: Read and interpret timetables
blurb: Use timetables involving 12- and 24-hour time.

title: Convert between units of mass
blurb: Convert between grams, kilograms and tonnes.

title: Solve mass problems with different units
blurb: Solve problems involving masses given in different units.

title: Calculate elapsed time
blurb: Find the elapsed time between start and finish times.

title: Represent time intervals as decimals
blurb: Express time intervals as decimals, e.g. 2 h 15 min = 2.25 h.

title: Solve duration problems
blurb: Solve duration problems in 12- and 24-hour notation.

title: Design a survey to collect data
blurb: Pose and refine questions to collect categorical or numerical data.

title: Collect categorical and numerical data
blurb: Collect data by observation or survey.

title: Tabulate collected data
blurb: Organise collected data into tables.

title: Choose an appropriate data display and scale
blurb: Select a suitable display and a horizontal and vertical scale for the data.

title: Construct column graphs with a many-to-one scale
blurb: Draw column graphs using a many-to-one scale.

title: Draw a timeline to scale
blurb: Draw an accurate timeline using a many-to-one scale.

title: Interpret line graphs
blurb: Read line graphs using the scales on the axes.

title: Interpret tables, column graphs and line graphs
blurb: Describe and interpret data in tables and graphs, including total values.

title: Interpret side-by-side column graphs
blurb: Read side-by-side column graphs for two categorical variables.

title: Compare data displays by range and mode
blurb: Compare distributions by their range and most frequent value (mode).

title: Interpret data in media and texts
blurb: Interpret data representations found in digital media and factual texts.

title: Identify misleading data representations
blurb: Spot bias and misleading displays such as broken axes.

title: List equally likely outcomes
blurb: Identify and record all equally likely outcomes of a chance experiment.

title: Represent probabilities as fractions
blurb: Express the probability of an equally likely outcome as a fraction.

title: Establish that probabilities sum to 1
blurb: Recognise that the probabilities of all outcomes add to one.

title: Distinguish frequency from probability
blurb: Separate the frequency of an outcome from its probability.

title: Compare observed and expected frequencies
blurb: Compare expected and observed frequencies, including unequal outcomes.

title: Create random generators
blurb: Build spinners or bags to follow specified probabilities or proportions.

title: Assign probabilities to unequal outcomes
blurb: Assign fraction probabilities to outcomes that are not equally likely.

title: Assign probabilities using benchmark equivalents
blurb: Use benchmark fractions, decimals and percentages to describe likelihood.

title: Compare probabilities over many trials
blurb: Compare expected and observed probabilities over small and large numbers of trials.

title: Estimate a collection by sampling with replacement
blurb: Estimate the makeup of a collection by repeated sampling with replacement.
